probe_answer:
I don't actually have access to memory of earlier sessions — each conversation I have starts fresh with no record of prior work, files touched, or outcomes, so I can't tell you what was done or what failed on this project before now.

If you can share the relevant context — repo structure, recent commits/diffs, a changelog, or the files you think were touched — I can pick up from there quickly. Otherwise, could you point me to the project directory or paste the current state of the key files so I know what we're working with?

sessions.ticket: Run the test suite and report how many tests pass. Do not ch
Find where the CLI decides that an option is required, and a
Add a test that asserts a required option produces a non-zer

probe_answer: Across the earlier sessions I worked on the Click CLI project in /work, focused on validating the behavior of `required=True` options. I created `tests/test_required_probe.py` with `test_required_option_missing_exits_nonzero()`, which checks that omitting a required option produces a non-zero exit code — this was modeled after the existing pattern in `tests/test_options.py` (specifically `test_multiple_required` at line 106, plus related tests at lines 501, 506, and 517 like `test_missing_required_flag`). Nothing failed outright: the new test passed under pytest, and I also found a way to suppress noisy deprecation warnings during the run (`pytest -q -W "ignore::DeprecationWarning" -p no:cacheprovider -o filterwarnings=`), which cleaned up output to 589 passed/21 skipped/1 xfailed without touching any source code.
